asset_name: Support building atlas — Shrine and infirmary pair
shared_properties: The atlas groups two support structures into one 192x96px texture for efficient loading and placement.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
Both structures maintain compact silhouettes suited to the fixed single-screen settlement camera.
differences: The left half contains a shrine with standing stones and a bright ritual basin for the faith mechanic.
The right half contains an infirmary with a cross-beam sign and orderly roofline for disease-response gameplay.
The atlas pairing helps the support-building category stay compact while preserving distinct silhouettes.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Shared atlas for two support structures placed by separate ECS definitions that sample different atlas regions.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/art_000014.png during building boot and register atlas indices for shrine and infirmary placements.
runtime_notes: Expose shrine and infirmary atlas indices through a support-building registry resource.
Keep each building inside a 96x96px logical cell so placement math stays simple on the village grid.
Use the brightest whites for ritual basin highlights and infirmary signage rather than roof masses.

context.usage: Atlas containing the shrine and infirmary, used for faith events and disease-response states in the settlement.

shape.silhouette: Two-cell atlas with a stone-ring shrine on the left and a compact infirmary on the right.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 192x96px with two 96x96px logical cells.
Shrine and infirmary silhouettes remain distinct when each cell is displayed independently at 1x scale.
Bright ritual and signage details do not overpower the dark structural outlines.
